scope: Windmill — developer platform: scripts, flows, schedules, apps

personas.name: Developer
personas.description: A new developer signing in to Windmill for the first time to write and schedule a small script.

personas.name: Admin
personas.description: Workspace admin: can manage users, groups, settings within a workspace.

personas.name: Operator
personas.description: Read-only / run-only workspace member: can execute scripts/flows/apps but not create or edit.

personas.name: Superadmin
personas.description: Instance-level superadmin: manages global users, instance groups, instance settings.

nodes.title: Script detail with auto-generated run UI

provenance.note: title 'Workspace Settings | Windmill'; h1 'Workspace settings: <workspace-id>'. Tabs are toggled via URL query (?tab=general|users|ai|premium|git|integrations|hooks|data|advanced); default landing tab on /workspace_settings shows the Users tab (h2 'Members (<n>)' + h2 'Operator settings'). The General tab (h2 'General') has Workspace name / id / color sections. Admin-only edit gates fire both client-side ({#if $userStore?.is_admin || $superadmin}) and server-side (workspaces.rs require_admin).

title 'Groups | Windmill'; h1 'Groups'. Primary action button labelled 'New group' (NOT 'Create group' or 'Add group'). Group rows show a member-count chip (e.g. '(0)') next to the name. Lists workspace groups and instance groups (SCIM-managed).
title 'Variables | Windmill'; h1 'Variables'. Primary create CTA is labelled 'New variable' (NOT 'Add variable'). View has 'Workspace' (default workspace-scoped) and 'Contextual' tab-toggles.

title 'Resources | Windmill'; h1 'Resources'. Tabs: 'Workspace' (resources, default), 'Resource Types' (admin), 'States', 'Cache', 'Theme'. Create CTAs: 'Add resource' for a new resource, 'Add resource type' for a new type (NOT 'New resource' / 'Create resource type').

nodes.title: Flows list (workspace Flows tab on Home)

provenance.note: Windmill does NOT expose a standalone /flows route — GET /flows 404s. The 'flow list' is the Flows sub-tab of the Home page workspace view (rendered by ItemsList.svelte with subtab='flow'), reached via the URL '/?kind=flow#workspace' or by clicking the 'Flows' ToggleButton on Home. Inferred URL shape from the Home page query/subtab plumbing and the existing dev-onboards journey's home-tab vocabulary; pre-walk before relying on it for selectors.

Two routes share the same FlowBuilder.svelte: /flows/add (redirects via makeDraftAddLoad to /flows/edit/u/<owner>/draft_<uuid>?new_draft=true) and /flows/edit/[...path]. The empty /flows/add/+page.svelte is intentional — the sibling +page.ts redirects at the load phase before mount. Confidence 'documented'; selector vocabulary (Deploy button label, node-add affordances) needs a walk before tests.

nodes.title: Flow detail with auto-generated run UI + graph viewer

provenance.note: Mirrors the script detail page: header with Edit / Runs / History / Fork / Build app buttons; left pane is a RunForm built from flow.schema; bottom pane is a FlowGraphViewer of flow.value.modules. Submitting the RunForm calls JobService.runFlowByPath and goto's /run/<jobId>.

Sidebar entry: TRIGGERS group, link label 'HTTP' (icon Route), href '/routes'. PageHeader title is 'Custom HTTP routes'; primary CTA is 'New route' (button text uses a non-breaking space); secondary buttons 'From OpenAPI spec' and 'To OpenAPI spec'. Rows render the route's HTTP method + canonical URL ('/<workspace_id>/<route_path>' when workspaced or instance-wide route prefix is enabled, else '/<route_path>'), the trigger path, the target runnable, an enable/suspend TriggerModeToggle, a Copy URL button (copies '/api/r/...' via getHttpRoute), an Edit/View button, and a dropdown with Suspend / Edit / Deploy to prod/staging / Audit logs / Permissions / Delete. Each list row's Edit opens the RouteEditor drawer in place (no /routes/edit/<path> route).

title 'Folders | Windmill'; h1 'Folders'. Sidebar group 'Folders & Groups' → link 'Folders' (href=/folders). Primary CTA is a Popover button labelled 'New folder'; clicking it reveals an input with placeholder 'New folder name' and a 'Create' submit button. Empty-state copy is 'No folders yet, create one'. Rows render Name (with optional Summary line), Labels, Scripts/Flows/Apps/Schedules/Variables/Resources usage counts, Participants chip, and a kebab Dropdown with 'Manage folder' and 'Delete' items. Clicking a row OR 'Manage folder' opens the FolderEditor in a Drawer whose title is 'Folder <name>'.

A Drawer (not a route) rendered on /folders with title 'Folder <name>'. Sections: 'Summary' (TextInput + Save), 'Labels' (LabelsInput), 'Permissions (<n>)' with a ToggleButtonGroup 'User|Group' + Select of usernames/groups + 'Grant' button (calls GranularAclService.addGranularAcls under the hood). The members table renders each owner_name with a Viewer/Writer/Admin ToggleButtonGroup (switches role via FolderService.addOwnerToFolder / removeOwnerToFolder) and a trash button (cannot remove yourself). For admins / wm_deployers, a CollapseLink 'Default permissioned as (advanced, prod only)' exposes a per-folder default_permissioned_as rules editor.

A Drawer (not a route) rendered on /routes. openNew(is_flow) / openEdit(path, is_flow) drive it; the drawer body has sections 'Summary' (text input), 'Path' (Path widget, kind='http_trigger'), 'Description', 'Target' (Runnable / Static asset / Static website ToggleButtonGroup; runnable picked via ScriptPicker), and 'HTTP' (route_path TextInput with placeholder pattern, http_method ToggleButtonGroup with labels 'GET' 'POST' 'PUT' 'PATCH' 'DELETE', workspaced_route toggle, computed 'Production URL'). Save calls saveHttpRouteFromCfg and then closes the drawer; backend conflict 'A route already exists with this path' is raised from check_if_route_exist when (route_path_key, http_method, [workspace_id]) already exists.

edges.label: Save → modal closes; row visible at /schedules
Click row OR 'New folder' → opens Folder drawer in place

features.name: Workspaces
features.summary: Workspaces are the unit of multi-tenancy in Windmill: each user belongs to one or more workspaces (each with its own scripts, flows, schedules, users, and settings), and every (logged) feature route is scoped to the workspace currently held in localStorage. A fresh user is routed to /user/workspaces until one is selected or created; non-admin users on Community Edition are capped at 2 workspaces beyond the default 'admins'; settings edits require workspace-admin.

flows.title: Create a new workspace from the picker and land on Home
flows.story: As a new developer with no workspace yet, I click '+ Create a new workspace' on /user/workspaces, submit a workspace name and ID, and land on the workspace home so I can use feature routes.
steps: On /user/workspaces (title 'Workspace Selection | Windmill', h1 'Select a workspace'), click the link '+ Create a new workspace'
On /user/create_workspace (h1 'New Workspace'), fill 'Workspace name' with 'Acme'
Fill 'Workspace ID' with a slug matching ^\w+(-\w+)*$ (e.g. 'acme-<rand>')
Click the button 'Create workspace'
Land on / (Workspace home) — localStorage.workspace is now set to the chosen ID

scenarios.title: Happy path — create workspace with valid name and slug ID
scenarios.statement:
Given I am a signed-in developer with no workspace selected
And I am on /user/workspaces
When I click the '+ Create a new workspace' link
And I fill 'Workspace name' with 'Acme'
And I fill 'Workspace ID' with 'acme-<rand>'
And I click 'Create workspace'
Then I land on /
And localStorage.workspace equals 'acme-<rand>'

scenarios.title: Workspace ID must match slug regex ^\w+(-\w+)*$
scenarios.statement:
Given I am on /user/create_workspace
When I fill 'Workspace ID' with 'Invalid ID!'
Then the form rejects the value and the 'Create workspace' button is disabled

scenarios.title: Community Edition caps non-admin users at 2 workspaces outside 'admins'
scenarios.statement:
Given I am a non-superadmin on Community Edition
And I already own 2 workspaces outside of 'admins'
When I POST /api/workspaces/create with a new workspace
Then the request is rejected with HTTP 400
And the error reads 'You have reached the maximum number of workspaces (2 outside of default workspace \'admins\') without an enterprise license.'

flows.title: Pick an existing workspace from the picker
flows.story: As a developer with one or more existing workspaces, I open /user/workspaces and click a workspace tile to select it, landing on Home with that workspace as the active one.
steps:
On /user/workspaces (h1 'Select a workspace'), under the h2 'Workspaces' section, see one button per workspace I belong to (accessible name pattern e.g. 'Admins
-
admins
as superadmin …')
Click the workspace tile button for the workspace I want
Land on / — localStorage.workspace is set to that workspace's slug

scenarios.title: Selecting a workspace tile persists it and routes to Home
scenarios.statement:
Given I am signed in and I belong to workspace 'admins'
And I am on /user/workspaces
When I click the workspace tile labeled 'Admins - admins as superadmin'
Then I land on /
And localStorage.workspace equals 'admins'

flows.title: Accessing a (logged) feature route without localStorage.workspace redirects to the picker
flows.story: As a developer whose browser has no localStorage.workspace yet (fresh sign-in or cleared storage), I navigate to any (logged) feature route and am redirected to /user/workspaces to select or create one first.
steps: Clear localStorage.workspace (and sessionStorage.workspace)
Navigate to any (logged) feature route (e.g. /scripts/add, /, /runs, /schedules, /workspace_settings)
Be redirected to /user/workspaces?rd=<URL-encoded-intended-route> (h1 'Select a workspace')

scenarios.title: Empty workspaceStore redirects every (logged) route to the picker
scenarios.statement:
Given I am signed in
And localStorage.workspace is empty
When I navigate to /scripts/add
Then I land on /user/workspaces?rd=/scripts/add

flows.title: Edit workspace name and color from Workspace Settings
flows.story: As a workspace admin, I open /workspace_settings → General, change the workspace name and color, and see the new values reflected in the workspace selector and sidebar.
steps: From Home, navigate to /workspace_settings?tab=general (sidebar Settings → Workspace, or direct URL)
Confirm h1 'Workspace settings: <workspace-id>' and h2 'General'
Change the 'Workspace name' field (e.g. to 'Acme Renamed')
Optionally change 'Workspace color' / 'Workspace ID'
Save — the change is recorded server-side (audit_log kind=Update), and the workspace selector reflects the new name and color

scenarios.title: Only workspace admins may edit name/color
scenarios.statement:
Given I am signed in as a non-admin member of workspace 'acme'
When I navigate to /workspace_settings
Then the 'Workspace name' input is not editable (admin-only sections are hidden)
scenarios.skipReason: non-admin user not in current SEED (single admin persona); needs a P2 grow to seed a non-admin member.

scenarios.title: Admin can rename the workspace and audit_log records the change
scenarios.statement:
Given I am a workspace admin on /workspace_settings
When I change the workspace name to 'Acme Renamed' and Save
Then POST /api/w/<id>/workspaces/change_workspace_name succeeds with 200
And an audit log entry of kind Update is recorded

flows.title: Add a user to the workspace (admin)
flows.story: As a workspace admin, I open Workspace Settings → Users, click 'Add new user', enter an email and pick a role, and the new member or pending invite is visible on the Users tab.
steps: On /workspace_settings?tab=users (default landing tab; h2 'Members (<n>)' + 'Operator settings')
Click the 'Add new user' button
Enter the user's email and pick a role (operator / developer / admin)
Submit the dialog — the action either adds the user immediately or sends an invite, depending on whether the email belongs to an existing Windmill user
On success the new row references the email in the page (member row or 'Invites' section)

scenarios.title: Non-admins cannot invite users
scenarios.statement:
Given I am a non-admin operator in workspace 'acme'
When I POST /api/w/acme/workspaces/invite_user
Then the response is HTTP 403
scenarios.skipReason: non-admin user not in current SEED; needs P2 grow for a non-admin persona.

scenarios.title: Adding a member to the 'admins' workspace requires an Enterprise license
scenarios.statement:
Given Windmill is running on Community Edition
And I am a superadmin
When I POST /api/w/admins/workspaces/invite_user with a non-admin email
Then the response is HTTP 400 with an EE-only error message
scenarios.skipReason: Community Edition error on inviting a non-admin into the 'admins' workspace surfaces as an HTTP 400 toast; reliably asserting the toast text requires verifying the exact CE error message format on the Users tab — deferred until covered by an admin-permission grow.

provenance.note: Updated 2026-06-23 by verify-feature: spec previously specified an 'Invite user' button + dedicated Invite dialog; the current Windmill build instead exposes a single 'Add new user' button that opens a unified add-or-invite dialog. The spec'd happy-path test (W05) was authored against the old label and failed.

flows.title: Leave a workspace (non-admin member)
flows.story: As a NON-ADMIN member of a workspace I no longer need, I leave it from Workspace Settings; admin owners cannot leave their own workspace and must archive or delete it instead.
steps: Precondition: I am a non-admin member of the workspace (not the owner / admin)
From /workspace_settings, click the 'Leave workspace' button (only rendered for non-admin members)
Confirm the destructive action
POST /api/w/<id>/workspaces/leave returns 200; land on /user/workspaces; the workspace is no longer in my list

scenarios.title: A non-admin member may leave the workspace
scenarios.statement:
Given I am a NON-ADMIN member of workspace 'acme'
When I POST /api/w/acme/workspaces/leave
Then the response is HTTP 200
And my user row in workspace 'acme' is removed

provenance.note: Updated 2026-06-23 by verify-feature: original spec story 'As a member' over-generalized; verified that admin owners cannot leave their own workspace (no Leave button rendered) — the product is correct, the spec needed narrowing.

flows.skipReason: needs a non-admin member persona — the current SEED only provisions the admin owner, for whom the 'Leave workspace' button (correctly) does not render. Request a P2 grow to seed a non-admin member, then re-enable this flow's test.

flows.title: Archive a workspace (admin)
flows.story: As a workspace admin, I open /workspace_settings → Advanced and archive the workspace; subsequent (logged) routes for that workspace become inaccessible until a superadmin unarchives it.
steps: On /workspace_settings?tab=advanced, click 'Archive workspace'
Confirm the destructive action — POST /api/w/<id>/workspaces/archive returns 200
Land on /user/workspaces — the workspace is hidden from non-superadmin pickers; superadmin can unarchive via POST /workspaces/unarchive/<id>

scenarios.title: Archive requires workspace-admin
scenarios.statement:
Given I am a non-admin member of workspace 'acme'
When I POST /api/w/acme/workspaces/archive
Then the response is HTTP 403
scenarios.skipReason: non-admin user not in current SEED; needs P2 grow.

scenarios.title: A superadmin can unarchive a workspace
scenarios.statement:
Given workspace 'acme' is archived
And I am a superadmin
When I POST /workspaces/unarchive/acme
Then the response is HTTP 200
And 'acme' reappears in the workspace picker for its members
scenarios.skipReason: superadmin unarchive is observable as the workspace reappearing in the picker — deferred (need a stable archived-precondition seed).

vocabulary.workspace-picker:
title 'Workspace Selection | Windmill'; h1 'Select a workspace'; h2 sections 'Workspaces' and 'Invites to join a Workspace'; existing-workspace tile is a button whose accessible name is e.g. 'Admins
-
admins
as superadmin Used to manage your Windmill instance' or 'Acme
-
<slug>
as admin'; link '+ Create a new workspace' (href=/user/create_workspace); page-level buttons 'Instance settings' and 'Log out'.
vocabulary.workspace-create: title 'Windmill'; h1 'New Workspace'; <CenteredModal> rendered as its own route /user/create_workspace; fields 'Workspace name' (autofocus) and 'Workspace ID' (slug — regex ^\w+(-\w+)*$ enforced client-side); optional 'Workspace color', 'AI key for Windmill AI', 'Auto-add anyone joining the instance' (admins-only); submit button 'Create workspace'; back link '← Back to workspaces'; success → goto('/') and persists localStorage.workspace = <id>.
vocabulary.workspace-settings: title 'Workspace Settings | Windmill'; h1 'Workspace settings: <workspace-id>'. Tab selection lives in URL query (?tab=general|users|ai|premium|git|integrations|hooks|data|advanced). Default landing on /workspace_settings (no ?tab) is the Users view: h2 'Members (<n>)' + h2 'Operator settings'. General tab (?tab=general): h2 'General' with Workspace name / ID / color editors. Admin-only sections render only when $userStore?.is_admin || $superadmin.
vocabulary.noWorkspaceRedirect: Navigating to any (logged) feature route (/, /scripts/add, /scripts/edit/u/.../draft_..., /runs, /schedules, /workspace_settings) without localStorage.workspace redirects to /user/workspaces?rd=<URL-encoded-intended-route>. Empirically observed: GET /scripts/add → /user/workspaces?rd=%2Fscripts%2Fedit%2Fu%2Fme%2Fdraft_<uuid>%3Fnew_draft%3Dtrue (note that /scripts/add already redirects internally to the draft URL before the workspace guard fires).

corrections: workspace-create page title is 'Windmill' (not 'New Workspace | Windmill') — the route renders the bare CenteredModal without overriding the document title.
/workspace_settings tabs are NOT exposed as role='tab' elements — they are clickable buttons/links in the sidebar of the page; tab selection is keyed by ?tab=<slug> in the URL.
Default tab on /workspace_settings (no query) is Users, not General — visit /workspace_settings?tab=general explicitly for the General editor.

features.name: Scripts (Python/TS/Go/Bash editor & runs)
features.summary: Author a script in the Monaco editor, pick a language, and deploy it to a workspace path; deploy lands on the script detail with an auto-generated run UI.

provenance.note: Seeded from journey dev-onboards-script-to-schedule decomposition; full feature spec to be authored in P1.

flows.title: Author and deploy a Python script from the workspace home
flows.story: As a developer in a fresh workspace, I open the Script editor from Home, pick Python, replace the body with a no-arg hello-windmill script, and Deploy — landing on the script detail page with its auto-generated run UI.
steps: From Home, click the 'Script' CTA — /scripts/add redirects to /scripts/edit/u/<owner>/draft_<uuid>
In the Language picker, click 'Python'
Replace the editor body with `def main():\n    return 'hello windmill'` and wait for 'Saved'
Click the 'Deploy' button — land on /scripts/get/<scriptPath>

flows.title: Author and deploy a TypeScript (bun) script from Home
flows.story: As a developer, I open the Script editor from Home and pick the TypeScript (bun) language; the editor body switches to the bun template, and after replacing it with a no-arg hello-windmill script I Deploy and land on the script detail page with its auto-generated run UI.
steps: From Home, click the 'Script' CTA — lands on /scripts/edit/u/<owner>/draft_<uuid> with Python selected by default
In the Language picker, click the 'TypeScript (Bun)' tile — the editor body is replaced with the bun template
Replace the editor body with `export async function main() { return 'hello windmill' }` and wait for the 'Autosave status' to read 'Saved'
Click the 'Deploy' button — land on /scripts/get/<scriptPath> with the auto-generated run form visible

scenarios.title: Switch language to TypeScript (bun) and deploy a hello script
scenarios.statement:
Given I am a developer on the Script editor from Home
When I click the 'TypeScript (Bun)' tile in the Language picker
And I replace the editor body with a no-arg hello-windmill bun script
And I click the 'Deploy' button
Then I land on the /scripts/get/<path> detail page
And the auto-generated run form is visible

flows.title: Edit an existing script's body and re-deploy to create a new version
flows.story: As a developer, I open a previously deployed script's detail page, click Edit to land on the editor at its workspace path, modify the body, click Deploy again, and the script detail page reflects the new content with a new hash recorded in version history.
steps: On the script detail page (/scripts/get/<path>), click the 'Edit' button — navigate to /scripts/edit/<path>
Modify the editor body (e.g. change the return string) and wait for 'Saved'
Click 'Deploy' — a confirmation modal may appear if other deployers have edits to merge
Land back on /scripts/get/<path> with the new body in effect; a new hash entry is recorded in the version history

scenarios.title: Re-deploying an existing script lands on the detail page with the new body
scenarios.statement:
Given I have a deployed script at 'u/admin/scripts-s03-<rand>' returning 'v1'
When I open its detail page and click Edit
And I change the body to return 'v2'
And I click Deploy
Then I land on the script detail page for that path
And the editor of that script shows the new body returning 'v2'

features.name: Flows (multi-step workflows)
features.summary: Flows are multi-step workflows that chain script steps, sub-flows, for/while loops, branches, approval/prompt steps, and AI agents in a visual graph. Authored in FlowBuilder (a drag-and-drop builder at /flows/add and /flows/edit/<path>) and persisted via FlowService.createFlow / updateFlow as a workspace-pathed record (POST /api/w/<id>/flows/create, /update/<path>). Deployed flows are listed on the Home Flows sub-tab (Windmill has no standalone /flows route), and each has its own detail page at /flows/get/<path> with an auto-generated RunForm built from the flow's input schema and a FlowGraphViewer rendering of its modules. Triggering a deployed flow POSTs /api/w/<id>/jobs/run/f/<path> and navigates to /run/<jobId>, where FlowProgressBar + FlowStatusViewer surface per-step status as the job executes. Operators cannot create flows (server-side 403); creates on Cloud are capped at 1000 per workspace.

provenance.note: Documented from code only — not yet walked. Selector vocabulary (e.g. exact Deploy button label, node-pickers, list-row delete affordance, success toasts) needs a walk before tests. The flows feature differs from scripts in two structural ways worth flagging: (1) there is NO /flows list route — the list lives on Home as the Flows sub-tab (ItemsList with subtab='flow', URL '/?kind=flow#workspace'); (2) the Home 'Flow' CTA opens a 'Create a new flow' modal (Flow Editor / Workflow-as-Code / Pipeline) rather than navigating straight to the editor.

flows.title: Author and deploy a 2-step Python flow from Home
flows.story: As a developer in a fresh workspace, I open the Flow editor from Home, add a first Python step that returns a value, add a second Python step that echoes its parent step's result back, and click Deploy — landing on /flows/get/<flowPath> with the auto-generated RunForm and the graph viewer of my two-step flow.
steps: From Home, click the 'Flow' CTA (CreateActionsFlow) — the 'Create a new flow' modal opens with two tiles: 'Flow Editor' and 'Workflow-as-Code'
Click the 'Flow Editor' tile — navigate to /flows/add, which redirects via makeDraftAddLoad to /flows/edit/u/<owner>/draft_<uuid>?new_draft=true
From the FlowBuilder graph's '+' inserter, pick 'Action' → add an inline Python script step (step 1) whose body is `def main():\n    return 'hello'` and wait for autosave
Click the next '+' inserter and add a second inline Python step (step 2) whose body is `def main(prev):\n    return prev` with input_transform on `prev` referencing step 1's result
Click the 'Deploy' button (FlowBuilder → DeployButton) — FlowService.createFlow is called against /api/w/<w_id>/flows/create
Land on /flows/get/<flowPath> with the auto-generated RunForm and a FlowGraphViewer showing two ordered step nodes

scenarios.title: Deploy a 2-step Python flow and land on its detail page
scenarios.statement:
Given I am a developer on Home in a fresh workspace
When I click the 'Flow' CTA and pick 'Flow Editor' in the modal
And I land on /flows/edit/u/<owner>/draft_<uuid>?new_draft=true
And I add a first inline Python step returning 'hello'
And I add a second inline Python step echoing step 1's result
And I click the 'Deploy' button
Then FlowService.createFlow is called (POST /api/w/<w_id>/flows/create) and returns 201
And I land on /flows/get/<flowPath>
And the FlowGraphViewer renders two ordered step nodes

scenarios.title: Operators cannot create flows
scenarios.statement:
Given I am signed in as a workspace operator
When I POST /api/w/<w_id>/flows/create with a valid NewFlow payload
Then the response is HTTP 403 with message 'Operators cannot create flows for security reasons'
scenarios.skipReason: Operator persona not in current SEED; needs a P2 grow to seed an operator user.

flows.title: Run a deployed flow from its detail page
flows.story: As a developer on a deployed flow's detail page, I submit the auto-generated RunForm; the JobService.runFlowByPath call goto's /run/<jobId>, where the FlowProgressBar and FlowStatusViewer render the run completing with a Success status.
steps: On /flows/get/<flowPath>, fill the auto-generated RunForm (no args for a no-arg flow) and click the 'Run' button
The page calls JobService.runFlowByPath against POST /api/w/<w_id>/jobs/run/f/<flowPath> and goto's /run/<jobId>
On /run/<jobId>, verify the h1 'run/<jobId>' is visible
Verify the FlowProgressBar renders (job_kind === 'flow' branch) and the FlowStatusViewer shows per-step completion
Verify the job-status badge renders as the green Check (Success) once both steps complete

scenarios.title: Run a deployed flow and see Success
scenarios.statement:
Given I have a deployed flow at <flowPath>
And I am on /flows/get/<flowPath>
When I click the 'Run' button on the auto-generated RunForm
Then POST /api/w/<w_id>/jobs/run/f/<flowPath> returns 201 with a jobId
And I land on /run/<jobId>
And the FlowProgressBar renders the flow's modules
And the job-status badge ends as the green Check (Success)

flows.title: Edit an existing flow and re-deploy with a new step appended
flows.story: As a developer iterating on a deployed flow, I open the flow detail page, click 'Edit' to land on /flows/edit/<flowPath>, append a third inline Python step from the InsertModule popover, click Deploy, and the detail page reflects the new 3-step graph.
steps: On /flows/get/<flowPath> for a 2-step flow, click the 'Edit' button (DetailPageHeader → button labelled 'Edit') — navigate to /flows/edit/<flowPath>
From the trailing '+' inserter on the graph, pick 'Action' → add a third inline Python step
Wait for autosave, then click the 'Deploy' button — FlowService.updateFlow is called against /api/w/<w_id>/flows/update/<path>
Land back on /flows/get/<flowPath> with a 3-step FlowGraphViewer; a new entry is recorded in the flow's version history

scenarios.title: Re-deploy an existing flow with an added step
scenarios.statement:
Given I have a deployed 2-step flow at <flowPath>
When I open /flows/get/<flowPath> and click the 'Edit' button
And I land on /flows/edit/<flowPath>
And I add a third inline Python step from the InsertModule popover
And I click 'Deploy'
Then POST /api/w/<w_id>/flows/update/<flowPath> returns 200
And I land on /flows/get/<flowPath>
And the FlowGraphViewer now renders 3 ordered step nodes

flows.title: Delete a flow from its detail page menu
flows.story: As a developer cleaning up an unused flow, I open /flows/get/<flowPath>, open the actions menu, click 'Delete', and the flow is removed from the workspace (DELETE /api/w/<id>/flows/delete/<path>); subsequent GETs at the path 404.
steps: On /flows/get/<flowPath>, open the actions menu in the DetailPageHeader
Click the 'Delete' menu item (red, Trash icon) — FlowService.deleteFlowByPath is called against DELETE /api/w/<w_id>/flows/delete/<flowPath>
A 'Flow deleted' toast appears; the page goto's '/' (Home)
Verify the Flows sub-tab on Home (/?kind=flow#workspace) no longer lists <flowPath>

scenarios.title: Delete a flow and confirm it disappears from the workspace list
scenarios.statement:
Given I have a deployed flow at <flowPath>
And I am on /flows/get/<flowPath>
When I open the actions menu and click 'Delete'
Then DELETE /api/w/<w_id>/flows/delete/<flowPath> returns 200
And I land on '/'
And the Home Flows sub-tab no longer lists <flowPath>

flows.title: View a flow run's per-step status in the graph
flows.story: As a developer reviewing a flow run, I open /run/<jobId> for a completed flow job; the FlowStatusViewer renders the flow's sub-graph with per-module status (Success / Failure / Running) so I can see which step finished or failed.
steps: Trigger a flow run from /flows/get/<flowPath> — land on /run/<jobId>
Verify the FlowProgressBar renders (job_kind === 'flow' branch in run/+page.svelte)
Verify the FlowStatusViewer renders below, showing one status node per module in job.flow_status.modules
Verify each module node's status icon corresponds to JobStatusIcon's enum (green Check = Success, red X = Failure, yellow Play = Running, gray Ban = Canceled)

scenarios.title: Flow run detail shows per-step status nodes
scenarios.statement:
Given I just ran a 2-step flow at <flowPath> successfully
And I am on /run/<jobId> for that flow job
Then the FlowProgressBar is visible
And the FlowStatusViewer renders one node per module in job.flow_status.modules
And every module node's status icon is the green Check (Success)

features.name: Runs and job history
features.summary: Trigger a deployed script from its auto-generated Run form, watch the job land on the /run/<jobId> detail page, and discover/manage past jobs in the /runs history. The detail page renders the run status as a colored icon badge (green Check = Success, red X = Failure, gray Ban = Canceled, yellow Play = Running, violet/blue Hourglass = Suspended/Queued), shows the script's return value in a 'Result' panel, and exposes Cancel + 'Run again' actions. /runs is a filterable list (status / path / user / folder / tag) with columns Duration, Path, Triggered by, and (when wide enough) Tag.

flows.title: Run a deployed script from its auto-generated UI and see Success
flows.story: As a developer on a freshly deployed script, I submit the auto-generated Run form and land on the job-detail page, where the run renders as a green Check status badge and the 'Result' panel contains my script's return value.
steps: On /scripts/get/<scriptPath>, fill the auto-generated RunForm and click the 'Run' button
The page calls JobService.runScriptByHash (or runScriptByPath for hub scripts) and goto's /run/<jobId>
On /run/<jobId>, verify the h1 'run/<jobId>' is visible, the status renders as the green Check badge (Success), and the 'Result' panel contains the script's return value ('hello windmill')

scenarios.title: Run a deployed script and see the Success result
scenarios.statement:
Given I have a deployed script at <scriptPath> that returns 'hello windmill'
And I am on /scripts/get/<scriptPath>
When I click the 'Run' button on the auto-generated RunForm
Then I land on /run/<jobId>
And the h1 'run/<jobId>' is visible
And the job-status badge renders as the green Check (Success)
And the 'Result' panel contains 'hello windmill'

flows.title: Find a completed run in the runs history
flows.story: As a developer who just ran (or scheduled) a script, I navigate to /runs from the sidebar and see at least one successful row for my script in the runs history table (rendered as a green Check icon link, not a literal 'Success' label).
steps: Navigate to /runs from the sidebar
Verify the page h1 'Runs' is visible
Verify the runs table renders columns 'Duration', 'Path', 'Triggered by'
Verify at least one row whose 'Path' column references <scriptPath> is visible, with a green Check status badge

scenarios.title: A completed run for my script appears in /runs
scenarios.statement:
Given I just ran script <scriptPath> successfully (jobId known)
When I navigate to /runs from the sidebar
Then the h1 'Runs' is visible
And the runs table renders columns 'Duration', 'Path', and 'Triggered by'
And a row whose Path column references <scriptPath> is visible
And that row's status badge is the green Check (Success)

provenance.note: Decomposition note: the journey verifier healed the runs-history assertion from /Success/i (no such label) to a green Check icon link — capture this on-screen vocabulary in the per-feature spec.

flows.title: Filter runs history by status
flows.story: As a developer triaging recent activity, I open the Status filter on /runs and pick 'Success' (or 'Failure', 'Running'); the runs table narrows to only matching rows.
steps: Navigate to /runs from the sidebar
Open the 'Status' filter in the FilterSearchbar and choose 'Success'
Verify the URL is updated (the filter is URL-synced) and that all visible rows render the green Check badge
Switch the Status filter to 'Failure' — only rows with the red X badge remain (or the empty-state 'No jobs found for the selected filters.' appears if none exist)

scenarios.title: Filtering by Status=Success narrows the runs list
scenarios.statement:
Given I am on /runs
And at least one successful run for <scriptPath> exists
When I open the 'Status' filter and pick 'Success'
Then every row visible in the runs table renders the green Check status badge
And a row referencing <scriptPath> is visible

scenarios.title: Status filter values are constrained to the documented enum
scenarios.statement:
Given the /runs page
When I open the 'Status' filter
Then the only available options are: All, Running, Success, Failure, Canceled, Waiting, Suspended
scenarios.skipReason: Pure UI-shape invariant on the Status filter enum (runsFilter.ts:161-169). Could be authored as a quick UI assertion but adds little over R03.S1; deferring until a P2 grow asks for an exhaustive-options check.

flows.title: Filter runs history by runnable path
flows.story: As a developer wanting to see only this script's runs, I either navigate to /runs/<scriptPath> directly or set the 'Path' filter on /runs; the table narrows to rows whose Path column matches that runnable.
steps: Navigate to /runs/<scriptPath> (the route's initialPath param pre-seeds the Path filter)
Verify the h1 'Runs' is visible and the 'Path' filter chip shows <scriptPath>
Verify every visible row's 'Path' column references <scriptPath>
Clear the Path filter — unrelated rows reappear

scenarios.title: Navigating to /runs/<scriptPath> seeds the Path filter
scenarios.statement:
Given I have a deployed script at <scriptPath> with at least one completed run
When I navigate to /runs/<scriptPath>
Then the h1 'Runs' is visible
And the Path filter is set to <scriptPath>
And every visible row's 'Path' column references <scriptPath>

flows.title: Cancel a running job from its detail page
flows.story: As a developer who launched a long-running script, I open /run/<jobId> while it is still running, click 'Cancel', and the job's status flips to the gray Ban (Canceled) badge.
steps: Launch a script that runs long enough to observe (e.g. one that sleeps a few seconds) from /scripts/get/<scriptPath> — land on /run/<jobId>
While the status badge is the yellow Play (Running), click the 'Cancel' button
The frontend POSTs /api/w/<w_id>/jobs_u/queue/cancel/<jobId>
After the cancel completes, the status badge flips to the gray Ban (Canceled)

scenarios.title: Cancel a running job from its detail page
scenarios.statement:
Given I am on /run/<jobId> and the job is still Running (yellow Play badge)
When I click the 'Cancel' button
Then POST /api/w/<w_id>/jobs_u/queue/cancel/<jobId> returns 200
And the status badge flips to the gray Ban (Canceled)

scenarios.title: Cancel button only renders while the job has not completed
scenarios.statement:
Given /run/<jobId> for a job whose state is 'CompletedJob'
When I view the action row
Then no 'Cancel' button is rendered

flows.title: Re-run a completed job from its detail page
flows.story: As a developer reviewing a completed run, I click 'Run again' on /run/<jobId> to re-execute the same runnable; the script-run page is reopened with the original args pre-filled, and an immediate 'Run immediately with same args' dropdown option queues a fresh job in one click.
steps: On /run/<jobId> for a completed script (job_kind == 'script' or 'flow'), locate the 'Run again' button
Click 'Run again' — the page goto's /scripts/get/<scriptHash>#<sharableArgsHash>, re-opening the auto-generated RunForm with the original args pre-filled
(Alternatively, open the 'Run again' dropdown and pick 'Run immediately with same args' to queue a new job without revisiting the form)
Submit the form — a new /run/<newJobId> page is loaded with its own status badge

scenarios.title: Re-run a completed script job via 'Run again'
scenarios.statement:
Given I am on /run/<jobId> for a completed script run of <scriptPath>
When I click the 'Run again' button
Then the page navigates to /scripts/get/<scriptHash>#<argsHash>
And the RunForm is pre-populated with the original args
When I click 'Run' on that form
Then I land on /run/<newJobId> for a new job whose script_path is <scriptPath>

flows.title: View a failed job and see the error in the Result panel
flows.story: As a developer debugging a broken script, I open /run/<jobId> for a failed run; the status renders as the red X badge and the 'Result' panel contains the error payload (DisplayResult renders the failure object).
steps: Run a script that throws an error (e.g. `throw new Error('boom')`) from /scripts/get/<scriptPath>
Land on /run/<jobId>
Verify the status badge is the red X (Failure)
Verify the 'Result' panel is visible and contains the error payload (the script's thrown message, as rendered by DisplayResult)

scenarios.title: A failed job shows the red X badge and an error in the Result panel
scenarios.statement:
Given I have a deployed script at <scriptPath> that throws 'boom'
When I run it from /scripts/get/<scriptPath>
And I land on /run/<jobId>
Then the status badge is the red X (Failure)
And the 'Result' panel is visible
And the rendered result contains 'boom'

features.name: Schedules (cron)
features.summary: Workspace schedules run a deployed script or flow on a cron expression. Schedules can be created from the Triggers panel of a script or from the standalone /schedules page; each schedule is workspace-scoped, has a unique path, a cron + timezone, an enabled toggle, optional pause-until, retry/error/recovery handlers, and produces queued jobs whose results show up in the runs history.

flows.title: Schedule a deployed script on a cron from its Triggers panel
flows.story: As a developer on a deployed script, I open Triggers → Add trigger → Schedule, set a cron in the schedule editor, and save — closing the editor with the schedule attached to the script.
steps: On /scripts/get/<scriptPath>, switch to the 'Triggers' tab in the script-detail panel
Click 'Add trigger' and pick 'Schedule' from the chooser
In the schedule editor, fill the Cron textbox with '* * * * *'
Click 'Save' — the schedule editor heading is hidden and the schedule is attached to the script

scenarios.title: Create a schedule from a script's Triggers panel
scenarios.statement:
Given I am on /scripts/get/<scriptPath> for a freshly deployed script
And the 'Triggers' tab is open
When I click 'Add trigger' and pick 'Schedule'
And I fill the Cron textbox with '* * * * *'
And I click 'Save'
Then the schedule editor closes
And a schedule with that cron is attached to <scriptPath>

scenarios.title: Creating a schedule with an invalid cron is rejected
scenarios.statement:
Given I am on the schedule editor for a deployed script
When I fill the Cron textbox with 'not a cron'
And I click 'Save'
Then the save fails with a cron-validation error
And no schedule row is created for that path

flows.title: See a created schedule listed on /schedules
flows.story: As a developer who just created a schedule, I navigate to /schedules from the sidebar and see a row whose cron expression matches what I saved.
steps: Navigate to /schedules from the sidebar
Verify the Schedules heading is visible
Verify a row referencing the saved cron expression is visible

scenarios.title: A newly created schedule appears in /schedules
scenarios.statement:
Given I created a schedule at 'u/admin/sc02-<rand>' on cron '* * * * *' for script <scriptPath>
When I navigate to /schedules from the sidebar
Then the Schedules heading is visible
And a row referencing 'u/admin/sc02-<rand>' with cron '* * * * *' is visible

flows.title: Create a schedule from the standalone /schedules page
flows.story: As a developer who already has a deployed script, I navigate to /schedules, click 'New schedule', pick the script, fill a cron, and save — a row for the new schedule appears in the list.
steps: From Home, navigate to /schedules (sidebar 'TRIGGERS' → 'Schedules'); confirm title 'Schedules | Windmill' and h1 'Schedules'
Click the 'New schedule' button — the schedule editor opens with sections 'Metadata', 'Schedule', 'Runnable', 'Advanced'
Under 'Runnable', click the 'Pick a script' input and select an existing deployed script
Under 'Schedule', fill the 'Cron' field (placeholder '0 0 */1 * * *') with '0 9 * * *' and leave the suggested 'Timezone' (e.g. 'Europe/Paris')
Under 'Metadata', leave the 'u/admin@windmill.dev' path prefix and type a unique name into the 'schedule' textbox (e.g. 'sc03-<rand>')
Click the 'Save' button — the editor closes
A row referencing 'u/admin@windmill.dev/sc03-<rand>' is visible in the schedules list

scenarios.title: Create a schedule via /schedules → New schedule
scenarios.statement:
Given I have a deployed script at <scriptPath>
And I am on /schedules
When I click 'New schedule'
And I pick <scriptPath> in the script picker
And I fill the Cron textbox with '0 9 * * *'
And I set the schedule path to 'u/admin/sc03-<rand>'
And I click 'Save'
Then a row with path 'u/admin/sc03-<rand>' and cron '0 9 * * *' is visible in /schedules

scenarios.title: Schedule path must be unique within the workspace
scenarios.statement:
Given a schedule at path 'u/admin/sc03-dup' already exists
When I POST /api/w/<id>/schedules/create with the same path
Then the response is HTTP 4xx (conflict / unique violation)
And no new schedule row is added
scenarios.skipReason: Backend-only invariant (POST /schedules/create with duplicate path returns 4xx via check_path_conflict in lib.rs:157-177). The /schedules editor auto-derives the path from the picked script and surfaces conflicts as a generic toast without a stable, UI-discipline-friendly affordance to assert against. Per playwright-ui-discipline, no UI assertion is faked. Leave to backend coverage.

flows.title: Toggle a schedule's enabled state from /schedules
flows.story: As a developer who owns a schedule, I flip its enabled toggle on the /schedules row; the cron immediately stops (or resumes) firing without otherwise modifying the schedule.
steps: On /schedules, locate the row of a schedule I own that is currently enabled
Click the enabled toggle in that row
The toggle flips to 'off' and the row reflects the disabled state
Click the toggle again — the schedule is re-enabled

scenarios.title: Disabling a schedule from the row toggle
scenarios.statement:
Given schedule 'u/admin/sc04-<rand>' is enabled on /schedules
When I click the enabled toggle on that row
Then POST /api/w/<id>/schedules/setenabled/u/admin/sc04-<rand> returns 200
And the row reflects the disabled state

scenarios.title: Non-owner cannot toggle another user's schedule
scenarios.statement:
Given schedule 'u/alice/sc04-<rand>' belongs to alice
And I am signed in as bob who lacks 'schedules:write:u/alice/sc04-<rand>'
When I POST /api/w/<id>/schedules/setenabled/u/alice/sc04-<rand>
Then the response is HTTP 403
scenarios.skipReason: non-owner persona not in current SEED — only admin@windmill.dev is seeded in sandbox/e2e/auth.setup.ts. Backend HTTP 403 from check_scopes('schedules:write:<path>') on /schedules/setenabled. Needs a P2 grow to seed a second non-admin member with their own storageState before this can be authored.

flows.title: Edit a schedule's cron from /schedules
flows.story: As a developer, I open an existing schedule from /schedules, change its cron expression in the editor, and save — the schedules list reflects the new cron.
steps: On /schedules, click the Edit affordance (row click or dropdown → Edit) for a schedule I own
The schedule editor opens pre-filled with the existing cron
Change the Cron textbox value (e.g. from '* * * * *' to '*/5 * * * *')
Click 'Save'
The editor closes and the row's cron column now shows '*/5 * * * *'

scenarios.title: Editing a schedule's cron
scenarios.statement:
Given schedule 'u/admin/sc05-<rand>' exists with cron '* * * * *'
And I am on /schedules
When I open its Edit affordance
And I change the Cron textbox to '*/5 * * * *'
And I click 'Save'
Then POST /api/w/<id>/schedules/update/u/admin/sc05-<rand> returns 200
And the row's cron column shows '*/5 * * * *'

flows.title: Delete a schedule from /schedules
flows.story: As a developer who owns a schedule, I delete it from /schedules; the row disappears and the cron stops firing.
steps: On /schedules, locate the row of a schedule I own
Open the row's dropdown / kebab menu and click 'Delete'
Confirm the destructive action
The row is no longer visible in /schedules

scenarios.title: Deleting a schedule removes its row
scenarios.statement:
Given schedule 'u/admin/sc06-<rand>' exists
And I am on /schedules
When I open the row's dropdown and click 'Delete'
And I confirm the destructive action
Then DELETE /api/w/<id>/schedules/u/admin/sc06-<rand> returns 200
And no row with that path is visible on /schedules

flows.title: Trigger 'Run now' on a schedule
flows.story: As a developer who owns a schedule, I trigger an on-demand run from the /schedules row's 'Run now' action; a job is queued immediately and appears in the runs history independently of the cron.
steps: On /schedules, locate the row of a schedule I own
Open the row's dropdown / kebab menu and click 'Run now'
A job is enqueued for the schedule's script/flow with its saved args and permissioned_as identity
Navigate to /runs and see a new row whose runnable matches the schedule's script_path

scenarios.title: Run now enqueues an immediate job
scenarios.statement:
Given schedule 'u/admin/sc07-<rand>' targets script <scriptPath>
And I am on /schedules
When I click 'Run now' in the row's dropdown
Then a new job for <scriptPath> appears in /runs within a few seconds

features.name: Users, groups and permissions
features.summary: Manage workspace membership and permissions: add/remove users, change per-workspace roles (Operator / Developer / Admin), create and edit groups, and toggle auto-add / auto-invite on email-domain match. Admin role required for all mutations; self-demotion is blocked; instance-level superadmin powers are global.

flows.title: Add a new user to the workspace (admin)
flows.story: As a workspace admin, I open Workspace Settings → Users, click 'Add new user', enter an email and pick a role (Operator/Developer/Admin), and the user is added or invited so they can access the workspace at that role level.
steps: From Home, navigate to /workspace_settings?tab=users (sidebar 'Settings' → 'Workspace' → Users)
Click the 'Add new user' button
Enter the user's email and pick a role (Operator, Developer, or Admin)
Submit — the row references the new user's email on the Users tab

scenarios.title: Admin adds an Operator-role user and the row appears
scenarios.statement:
Given I am an admin on /workspace_settings?tab=users
When I click 'Add new user'
And I fill the email with 'newop-<rand>@example.com' and pick 'Operator'
And I submit the dialog
Then a row referencing 'newop-<rand>@example.com' is visible in the Users table

scenarios.title: Non-admins cannot add users
scenarios.statement:
Given I am a non-admin member of workspace 'acme'
When I POST /api/w/acme/users/add
Then the response is HTTP 403
scenarios.skipReason: non-admin persona not in current SEED — backend HTTP 403 on /users/add. Needs a P2 grow to seed a non-admin member with their own storageState.

flows.title: Change a workspace user's role (admin)
flows.story: As a workspace admin, I open the Users tab and click a role chip (Operator / Developer / Admin) in the row of an existing member to change their workspace role; the row reflects the new role immediately.
steps: On /workspace_settings?tab=users, find the row of an existing non-admin member
Click the role chip you want to assign (Operator / Developer / Admin) on that row
The selected chip becomes the active selection on the row
POST /api/w/<id>/users/update/<username> records the new role

scenarios.title: Admin promotes an Operator to Developer
scenarios.statement:
Given I am an admin on /workspace_settings?tab=users
And user 'op@example.com' has the Operator role in this workspace
When I click the 'Developer' role chip in op@example.com's row
Then the 'Developer' chip becomes the active selection on that row

flows.title: Self-demotion prevention — admin cannot demote themselves
flows.story: As an admin viewing my own row on the Users tab, the role chips for Operator/Developer are disabled (or block my click) so I cannot accidentally demote myself out of admin and lock myself out of the workspace.
steps: On /workspace_settings?tab=users, find my own row
Attempt to click the 'Operator' or 'Developer' role chip on my own row
The click is rejected or the chips are visibly disabled — my row stays at 'Admin'

scenarios.title: Self-demotion is blocked
scenarios.statement:
Given I am an admin viewing /workspace_settings?tab=users
When I attempt to set my own row's role to 'Operator'
Then the change is rejected (UI disabled or 'Ask another admin' prompt)

flows.title: Create a workspace group
flows.story: As a workspace admin, I open the Groups page, click the 'New group' popover, enter a name, and the new group appears in the groups list.
steps: From Home, navigate to /groups (sidebar 'Folders & Groups' → Groups)
On /groups (title 'Groups | Windmill', h1 'Groups'), click the 'New group' button
Enter a unique group name in the popover's name input
Submit the popover — POST /api/w/<id>/groups/create returns 201
A new row referencing the new group's name is visible on /groups

scenarios.title: Admin creates a workspace group and it appears in the list
scenarios.statement:
Given I am an admin on /groups
When I open the new-group popover and enter 'engineers-<rand>'
And I submit the popover
Then a row referencing 'engineers-<rand>' is visible on the Groups page

scenarios.title: Creating a group with an existing name is rejected
scenarios.statement:
Given a group named 'engineers' already exists in workspace 'acme'
When I POST /api/w/acme/groups/create with name 'engineers'
Then the response is HTTP 4xx (conflict / unique violation)
scenarios.skipReason: duplicate-group conflict surfaces as an HTTP 4xx; relevant UI feedback (toast text) needs verification on the popover — deferred until covered by a P2 grow that pre-seeds a conflicting group.

flows.title: Add a member to a workspace group
flows.story: As a workspace admin, I open an existing group, add a workspace user as a member, and the member is listed in the group's roster.
steps: On /groups, click the row of an existing workspace group
In the group editor drawer, open the members section
Add an existing workspace user by email
POST /api/w/<id>/groups/adduser/<name> returns 200; the user is listed as a member

scenarios.title: Admin adds an existing workspace user to a group
scenarios.statement:
Given workspace 'acme' contains user 'op@example.com' and group 'engineers'
And I am an admin on /groups
When I open the 'engineers' group editor
And I add 'op@example.com' as a member
Then 'op@example.com' is listed under 'engineers' members in the page

flows.title: Delete a workspace group
flows.story: As a workspace admin, I delete a group from the Groups page; the group row disappears from the list.
steps: On /groups, locate the group I want to delete
Open the row's dropdown / kebab menu and click 'Delete'
Confirm the destructive action
DELETE /api/w/<id>/groups/delete/<name> returns 200; the group is gone from the list

scenarios.title: Admin deletes a group and it disappears from the list
scenarios.statement:
Given group 'engineers' exists in workspace 'acme'
And I am an admin on /groups
When I open the dropdown for 'engineers' and click 'Delete'
And I confirm the action
Then the 'engineers' row is no longer visible on /groups

flows.title: Toggle the auto-add (or auto-invite) setting for the workspace
flows.story: As a workspace admin, I open the Users tab and use the 'Auto-add: OFF/ON' toggle to control whether new users with a matching email domain are automatically added (or invited) to the workspace.
steps: On /workspace_settings?tab=users, find the 'Auto-add: OFF' or 'Auto-invite: OFF' button
Click it to open the popover
Toggle the master enable switch ON; optionally restrict by email domain and pick a default role
POST /api/w/<id>/settings/edit_auto_invite returns 200; the button now reads 'Auto-add: ON' (or 'Auto-invite: ON')

scenarios.title: Admin enables auto-add and the toggle reflects ON
scenarios.statement:
Given I am an admin on /workspace_settings?tab=users with auto-add off
When I open the 'Auto-add: OFF' popover
And I enable the master toggle and pick 'Operator' as the default role
And I save the popover
Then the toggle button now reads 'Auto-add: ON' (or 'Auto-invite: ON' in cloud mode)

vocabulary.groups: title 'Groups | Windmill'; h1 'Groups'. Create CTA labelled exactly 'New group' (popover with name input). Each group row shows a member-count chip e.g. '(0)' next to the name and a dropdown for edit/delete actions.
vocabulary.users-tab: On /workspace_settings?tab=users (default landing tab of /workspace_settings on non-default workspaces) — h2 'Members (<n>)', then per-row Operator/Developer/Admin role chips (a ToggleButtonGroup), a 'Reassign' button (admin → reassign-owner), and a 'Remove' button (offboarding). Bottom of page: h2 'Operator settings'. The add-user CTA is labelled 'Add new user' on workspaces where the admin can add users; on the seeded 'admins' workspace this button is suppressed (members must be added via /users/create at the instance level).
vocabulary.auto-add-toggle: Button labelled 'Auto-add: OFF' or 'Auto-invite: OFF' (depending on cloud vs on-prem mode); clicking opens a popover with a master toggle, optional domain restriction, default role chooser (Operator/Developer), and an Instance Groups subsection.

corrections: On the seeded 'admins' workspace, /workspace_settings?tab=users does NOT show the 'Add new user' button — adding members to 'admins' requires EE or the global /users/create endpoint. The button IS rendered on any user-owned workspace (verified on a freshly-created acme-* probe).
/groups uses 'New group' as the create CTA — not 'Create group' or 'Add group'.

features.name: Variables and resources
features.summary: Manage workspace-scoped Variables (small string values; optionally encrypted-secret) and Resources (typed JSON blobs validated against a workspace resource-type schema). Paths follow the u/<user>/, f/<folder>/, g/<group>/ ownership convention; secret variable values are masked in list responses and only decrypted on explicit GET with permission; resource types are admin-managed.

flows.title: Create a non-secret variable under u/<me>/
flows.story: As a developer, I create a non-secret variable scoped to my user path (u/<me>/<name>); the variable appears in the /variables list with its value visible (non-secret).
steps: From Home, navigate to /variables (sidebar Variables)
Click the 'New variable' / create-variable button
Fill the path field with 'u/<me>/<name>' (auto-validated against ^[ug](/[\w-]+){2,}$)
Leave 'Secret' OFF
Optionally fill description and labels
Submit — a row referencing the new variable's path is visible in the list with the value rendered

scenarios.title: Create a plaintext variable and the value is visible in the list
scenarios.statement:
Given I am a developer on /variables
When I create a non-secret variable at 'u/admin/vr01-<rand>' with value 'hello'
Then a row with path 'u/admin/vr01-<rand>' is visible in the variables table
And the value column for that row shows 'hello'

scenarios.title: Path must match the u/.../... format
scenarios.statement:
Given I am on the create-variable form

features.name: Folders
features.summary: Folders are workspace-scoped path namespaces under f/<name>/ that bundle scripts/flows/apps/schedules/variables/resources behind a shared ACL. The /folders page lists them, the FolderEditor drawer manages members (admin/writer/viewer) and optional default_permissioned_as overrides. Folder names match ^[a-zA-Z_0-9-]+$ (backend VALID_FOLDER_NAME). Membership is enforced by the backend: only owners ('admin' role) can add/remove other owners, and only owners can delete the folder; non-owners listing /folders/list will simply not see it (user_db RLS). Creating a folder via UI immediately opens its FolderEditor drawer for further setup.

Close the drawer — the new folder row is visible in the table (Name cell contains the folder name)

vocabulary.folders-list: title 'Folders | Windmill'; h1 'Folders'. The page CTA is a Popover trigger labelled 'New folder' (NOT 'Add folder' or 'Create folder'). Activating it reveals an input with placeholder 'New folder name' and a submit button labelled 'Create'. The DataTable head columns are Name / Labels / Scripts / Flows / Apps / Schedules / Variables / Resources / Participants / (kebab). Empty-state row text is 'No folders yet, create one'. Each row's last cell is a Dropdown with items 'Manage folder' (pencil icon, opens drawer) and 'Delete' (trash icon, calls FolderService.deleteFolder).
vocabulary.folder-editor: Drawer (NOT a route) opened in place on /folders. Drawer title is 'Folder <name>'. Sections: Label 'Summary' (TextInput placeholder 'Short summary…' + 'Save'); Label 'Labels' (LabelsInput chips); Label 'Permissions (<n>)' which on owner access shows an Alert 'New permissions may take up to 60s to apply', a User|Group ToggleButtonGroup, a Select to pick a username/group, and a 'Grant' button. The members table has a Viewer|Writer|Admin ToggleButtonGroup per row plus a trash button (disabled on self). When the folder does not exist yet, a yellow Alert 'Folder not found' is shown with a 'Create folder "<name>"' button. For admins / wm_deployers the CollapseLink 'Default permissioned as (advanced, prod only)' exposes a per-folder default_permissioned_as rules table.

corrections: The /folders create flow is NOT a separate Modal — it is a Popover bound to the 'New folder' button. The text input inside the popover has placeholder 'New folder name' (verbatim).

Submitting the popover does NOT just create the folder — it then automatically opens the FolderEditor drawer for further setup. Tests must close the drawer (Escape) before asserting on the table row to avoid the drawer overlaying the list.

The 'admin@windmill.dev' superadmin seeded in the 'admins' workspace has permissioned_as = 'admin@windmill.dev' (raw email, NOT 'u/admin'), because username_to_permissioned_as returns email values unchanged when they contain '@'. This is the value that appears in folder.owners after the UI creates a folder.

The FolderEditor 'Grant' button calls GranularAclService.addGranularAcls (which writes to folder.extra_perms with role=writer), NOT FolderService.addOwnerToFolder. To grant 'admin' (owner) role, the per-row Viewer|Writer|Admin ToggleButtonGroup must be flipped to 'Admin' (which then DOES call /folders/addowner). Tests asserting on /folders/get/<name> must check extra_perms (the granular permission grant) — not owners — after a 'Grant' click.

features.name: Triggers (HTTP routes & webhooks)
features.summary: Non-schedule entrypoints that invoke a deployed runnable. SCOPED to two trigger types that work out-of-the-box in the single-process Community Edition sandbox: (1) custom HTTP routes — managed at /routes, sidebar 'TRIGGERS' → 'HTTP'; admin-configurable path / method / target runnable; resolved at runtime by /api/r/<route_path> (or /api/r/<workspace_id>/<route_path> when workspaced_route is set or instance setting http_route_workspaced_route is true); (2) per-runnable webhooks — auto-generated on every deployed script/flow (no create UI), exposed via the Triggers tab on /scripts/get/:path and /flows/get/:path under a 'Webhooks' section that prints the canonical /api/w/<id>/jobs/run/p/<path> (async), /run_wait_result/p/<path> (sync), /run_and_stream/p/<path> (SSE) URLs. Out of scope on purpose: WebSockets, Postgres, Kafka, NATS, SQS, GCP Pub/Sub, Azure Event Grid, MQTT, Email — all of these require external infrastructure and most are Enterprise-only (Kafka/NATS/SQS/GCP/Azure are gated behind $enterpriseLicense in SidebarContent.svelte).

provenance.note: Authored P1 from code. The /routes page is the dedicated CRUD surface for HTTP routes; the per-runnable webhook URLs are read-only and surfaced only through the Triggers tab on script/flow detail pages — there is no /webhooks list. EE-gated trigger types (Kafka/NATS/SQS/GCP/Azure) and infrastructure-dependent ones (Postgres CDC, MQTT, WebSockets, Email) are deliberately excluded from authored flows.

flows.title: Create an HTTP route from /routes that targets a deployed script
flows.story: As a developer with a deployed script, I open /routes from the sidebar, click 'New route', pick the script as the runnable, set a unique route path and an HTTP method (default POST), and Save — a row for the new route appears in the list and its canonical URL ('/api/r/<workspace>/<route_path>') invokes the script when called.
steps: From Home, navigate to /routes (sidebar TRIGGERS → 'HTTP'); confirm PageHeader title 'Custom HTTP routes'
Click the 'New route' button — the RouteEditor drawer opens
In the drawer, fill 'Summary' with a short label and leave the 'Path' field at its auto-generated 'u/<me>/route-<rand>'
Under 'Target' (Runnable selected by default), use the ScriptPicker to pick an existing deployed script
Under 'HTTP', fill the 'Path' (route_path) text input with a unique slug (e.g. 't01-<rand>') and leave http_method on 'POST'
Click 'Save' — the drawer closes
A row referencing the trigger path appears at /routes with the canonical URL containing POST and the route_path

Each row shows method+URL (or summary), trigger path, runnable, a TriggerModeToggle, a 'Copy URL' button, an 'Edit'/'View' button, and a dropdown with: View Script/Flow, Suspend job execution, Edit/View, Deploy to prod/staging, Audit logs, Permissions, Delete.
vocabulary.route-editor: RouteEditor drawer (rendered on /routes, not on a /routes/add page). Sections: 'Summary' (input), 'Path' (workspace path with kind='http_trigger', namePlaceholder='route'), 'Description' (textarea), 'Target' (ToggleButtonGroup Runnable / Static asset / Static website; the Runnable mode uses ScriptPicker which allows scripts and flows), 'HTTP' (route_path TextInput with hint about ':param' and '*wildcard', http_method ToggleButtonGroup GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE, workspaced_route Toggle, computed 'Production URL'). vocabulary.webhook-panel: Per-runnable webhooks are read-only and exposed only via the Triggers tab on /scripts/get/<path> and /flows/get/<path>. The 'Webhooks' Section contains a description 'Webhooks trigger scripts or flows via HTTP requests.' and a WebhooksConfigSection that builds URLs from '<origin>/api/w/<workspace>/jobs/run/{p|f}/<path>' (async), '/run_wait_result/' (sync), '/run_and_stream/' (SSE). corrections: There is no /routes/add or /routes/edit/<path> route — the RouteEditor lives entirely in a drawer rendered by /routes/+page.svelte (and by the Triggers tab on script/flow detail pages).
The route-create CTA is labelled 'New route' (button uses 'New route' in markup), NOT 'Add route' or 'Create route'.

Webhooks have no dedicated sidebar entry under TRIGGERS — they are surfaced only on the runnable's Triggers tab. The TRIGGERS sidebar group lists only Schedules + the HTTP/WebSockets/Postgres/Kafka/NATS/SQS/GCP/Azure/MQTT/Email trigger pages plus 'used' native trigger kinds.

features.name: Apps (low-code UI builder)
features.summary: Windmill Apps are low-code drag-drop UI dashboards. SCOPED here to the low-code app surface (NOT raw apps): builder at /apps/edit/<path> (AppEditor), in-workspace viewer at /apps/get/<path> (InWorkspaceAppViewer wrapping PublicAppFrame, rendering inside an opaque /app_embed/<wid>/<path> iframe for sandboxed apps), JSON definition surfaced at /apps/get_raw_app/<path>. Backend CRUD endpoints (windmill-api/src/apps.rs L89-130): POST /apps/create, POST /apps/update/<path>, DELETE /apps/delete/<path>, GET /apps/get/p/<path>, GET /apps/list. CreateApp shape (L316): { path, summary, value: AppValue (open JSON), policy: Policy { execution_mode: publisher | viewer | anonymous, ... } }. The builder UI is drag-drop with hundreds of selectors and is prohibitively brittle to drive via Playwright — tests use the API-precondition + UI-assertion split: POST /apps/create with a serialized AppValue (the minimal accepted shape is { grid: [], hiddenInlineScripts: [], css: {}, norefreshbar: false }), then drive only the runtime viewer / list UI for assertions. Apps are listed on the Home page under the 'Apps' toggle (kind=app#workspace) — there is no dedicated /apps list route.

provenance.note: Authored P1 from code. The builder (AppEditor) is excluded from authored flows: the drag-drop authoring surface is too fragile to drive end-to-end. All flows use the API-precondition pattern (POST /apps/create with a serialized AppValue) and only drive the viewer / list UI for assertions.

flows.title: Create and deploy a minimal app via API; viewer mounts at /apps/get/<path>
flows.story: As a developer, I POST a minimal app value to /apps/create (the builder is too fragile to author end-to-end). The new row appears in the workspace; navigating to /apps/get/<path> mounts the InWorkspaceAppViewer (the parent Svelte page is on-origin, the app's own markup loads in the /app_embed iframe) and the page <title> reflects the app's path.
steps: API: POST /api/w/<wid>/apps/create with { path, summary, value: { grid: [], hiddenInlineScripts: [], css: {}, norefreshbar: false }, policy: { execution_mode: 'viewer' } } — backend returns 201 and the path
Navigate to /apps/get/<path>; the InWorkspaceAppViewer mounts (its #app-edit-btn wrapper renders once getAppLiteByPath resolves writer permission for the seeded admin)
The page <title> contains 'App <path>'
API: GET /api/w/<wid>/apps/get/p/<path> returns the deployed row with the posted summary

vocabulary.app-viewer: /apps/get/<path> is a thin wrapper (apps/get/[...path]/+page.svelte L1-32) over InWorkspaceAppViewer; it sets the document title to 'App <path>' via the +page.js load() returning stuff.title = `App ${params.path}`. The viewer mounts PublicAppFrame which either (a) renders the app inline on the real origin (unsandboxed low-code, raw) or (b) renders an opaque iframe at /app_embed/<wid>/<path>?... (sandboxed low-code). An 'Edit' button (Pen icon, '#app-edit-btn') is mounted in the bottom-right once getAppLiteByPath resolves and the user has writer perms.
vocabulary.app-editor: /apps/edit/<path> mounts AppEditor (apps/edit/[...path]/+page.svelte). The editor is the drag-drop builder — hundreds of selectors, autosave/diff/draft modals, multi-pane layout. The /apps/add route immediately redirects to /apps/edit/<owner>/draft_<uuid>?new_draft=true (emptyApp() seed). Deploy from this route calls AppService.createApp (draft-only) or AppService.updateApp (deployed + draft). Out of scope on purpose for authored flows.
vocabulary.apps-list: Apps are listed on the Home page under the 'Apps' toggle (CreateActionsApp + ToggleButtonGroup at +page.svelte L360-367, query ?kind=app#workspace). There is no dedicated /apps route; the sidebar has no 'Apps' entry — only the home-page toggle and the 'App' CTA in the header (CreateActionsApp).
vocabulary.app-value: AppValue is open-ended on the backend (sqlx::types::Json<Box<RawValue>>). The minimal shape the in-workspace viewer accepts and renders (derived from windmill/cli/test/mixed_case_paths.test.ts createApp() helper) is { grid: [], hiddenInlineScripts: [], css: {}, norefreshbar: false }. Builder-seeded apps (emptyApp() in editor/appUtils.ts L1325) include a topbar component plus DEFAULT_THEME, but neither is required for the backend to accept the value.

corrections: There is no /apps list route — apps are listed via the Home page's 'Apps' toggle (?kind=app#workspace). The sidebar does not have an 'Apps' entry.

The CreateApp.policy field is REQUIRED on the backend (it deserializes to Policy { execution_mode, ... }), even though execution_mode is the only field that must be set. A bare {} policy fails to deserialize.

The in-workspace viewer renders the app's markup inside an opaque /app_embed iframe (PublicAppFrame's sandbox-isolation path), so any click+result assertion against the app's own components crosses a sandboxed iframe boundary that is prohibitively brittle to drive in Playwright — hence the API-precondition + UI-mount-only assertion split in A01–A04.

features.name: Raw apps (HTML/JS bundles)
features.summary: Raw apps are user-authored HTML/JS bundles (React/Svelte/Vue starter templates exist; runtime is framework-agnostic) deployed as the same `app` row as low-code apps but with the `app_version.raw_app` boolean flipped to true. The frontend authoring surface is /apps_raw/add and /apps_raw/edit/<path> (RawAppEditor — Monaco-backed multi-file IDE with hundreds of selectors, out-of-scope for authored UI flows for the same reason A* flows skip the low-code builder). The viewer at /apps_raw/get/<path> is a thin wrapper around the same InWorkspaceAppViewer the low-code /apps/get route uses; it sets the document <title> to `App <path>` via +page.js. Backend (windmill/backend/windmill-api/src/apps.rs L89-130): CRUD lives on the apps router with multipart bodies — POST /apps/create_raw, POST /apps/update_raw/<path>, both fields { app (json), js, css? } (the js bundle is required; create_app_internal stores it in app_bundles keyed by app_version_id). DELETE /apps/delete/<path> and GET /apps/get/p/<path> are SHARED with low-code apps; the response includes `raw_app: true` for raw rows. The `windmill-api/src/raw_apps.rs` file is a separate read-only legacy router (GET /raw_apps/list, /raw_apps/get_data/{version}/{path}) backed by a `raw_app` table that is empty on a CE install — new raw apps go to the `app` table via /apps/create_raw, so /raw_apps/list returns [] for them. Update gotcha: for raw-app updates the EditApp `value` field MUST be supplied — update_app_internal only INSERTs a new app_version row when ns.value is Some(_); without it the multipart handler tries to re-upload the JS bundle against the prior version id and trips the app_bundles_pkey unique constraint (HTTP 400 SqlErr). CE-supported (verified against the live local stack: POST /apps/create_raw → 201, POST /apps/update_raw → 200 when `value` is set, DELETE → 200).

provenance.note: Authored P1 from code + a live multipart probe against the local CE stack (verified create_raw returns 201, update_raw returns 200 when `value` is set in the app JSON, delete is the shared DELETE /apps/delete/<path>). The RawAppEditor (drag/Monaco multi-file IDE) is excluded from authored flows for the same reason the low-code builder is in `apps` — hundreds of brittle selectors. All flows use the API-precondition + UI-mount-only assertion split.

flows.title: Create a raw HTML/JS app via /apps/create_raw and the viewer mounts at /apps_raw/get/<path>
flows.story: As a developer, I POST a minimal raw-app bundle (index.html + index.js) to /apps/create_raw. The row is persisted in the `app` table with `raw_app: true`; GET /apps/get/p/<path> returns the round-tripped bundle bytes; navigating to /apps_raw/get/<path> mounts the InWorkspaceAppViewer (the same viewer the low-code /apps/get route uses) and the document <title> reflects the app path.
steps: API: POST /api/w/<wid>/apps/create_raw multipart with `app` = { path, summary, value: { files: { 'index.html': { code }, 'index.js': { code } }, runnables: {} }, policy: { execution_mode: 'viewer' } }, `js` = the bundle, optional `css` — backend returns 201 and the path

API: GET /api/w/<wid>/apps/get/p/<path> returns raw_app=true, the posted summary, and value.files['index.html'].code containing the slug-stamped marker (round-trip proves the bytes the viewer would serve)
Navigate to /apps_raw/get/<path>; +page.svelte mounts InWorkspaceAppViewer keyed on `<wid>/<path>` and +page.js sets stuff.title = `App <path>`
The document <title> contains 'App <path>'

scenarios.title: Raw-app update_raw requires `value` in the app JSON (else app_bundles PK conflict)
scenarios.statement:
Given a raw app deployed at 'u/admin/ra02pk-<rand>'

Then the response is HTTP 400 with an app_bundles_pkey unique-constraint error (update_app_internal only inserts a new app_version when ns.value is Some, so the multipart handler tries to write the new JS against the prior version id)

provenance.note: Verified against the live local CE stack — POST update_raw without `value` returns HTTP 400 'SqlErr: error returned from database: duplicate key value violates unique constraint app_bundles_pkey'. The happy-path RA02 already documents the workaround (always set `value` on update); this scenario captures the negative invariant.

Asserting the exact SQL error message is brittle (depends on Postgres error wording) and the positive path RA02.S1 already proves the supported workflow end-to-end; deferring until a focused negative-tests grow.
