vocabulary.raw-app-viewer: /apps_raw/get/<path> mounts InWorkspaceAppViewer (same component as low-code /apps/get) keyed on `<workspace>/<path>` so SvelteKit fully remounts on in-route navigation. +page.js sets stuff.title = `App <path>`, so the on-origin observable signal is the document <title> (same as /apps/get).
vocabulary.raw-app-editor: /apps_raw/edit/<path> mounts RawAppEditor (Monaco-backed multi-file IDE; React/Svelte/Vue starter templates picked by RawAppTemplatePicker on `new_draft=true`). /apps_raw/add immediately redirects via makeDraftAddLoad('apps_raw/edit') to /apps_raw/edit/u/<owner>/draft_<uuid>?new_draft=true. Out of scope for authored flows.
vocabulary.raw-app-value: AppValue for raw apps is `{ files: Record<string, { code: string }>, runnables: Record<string, ...> }` (the React/Svelte/Vue starter templates in $lib/components/raw_apps/templates.ts seed this shape with index.html / index.tsx / etc). The minimal accepted shape for RA01-RA03 is `{ files: { 'index.html': { code }, 'index.js': { code } }, runnables: {} }`; the literal HTML/JS bytes round-trip via /apps/get/p/<path>.
vocabulary.raw-app-multipart: create_raw / update_raw bodies are multipart/form-data with parts `app` (application/json — CreateApp or EditApp), `js` (application/javascript, REQUIRED — the multipart handler errors `js or css file not uploaded` otherwise), `css` (text/css, optional). The js bytes are stored in `app_bundles` keyed by (app_version_id, file_type).

corrections: Raw apps and low-code apps share the SAME backend table (`app`) and the SAME GET /apps/get/p/<path> + DELETE /apps/delete/<path> handlers — only the create/update entry points are distinct (multipart create_raw/update_raw vs JSON create/update). The /raw_apps/list and /raw_apps/get_data/{version}/{path} routes in raw_apps.rs are a legacy read-only layer over a separate `raw_app` table that is empty on CE for apps created via the new flow.
There is no /apps_raw list route — raw apps surface alongside low-code apps in the Home page's Apps toggle (?kind=app#workspace).

/apps/get_data/v/{version}/{path} (the same-origin raw-app data endpoint that serves the JS bundle to RawAppPreview) expects the path-after-version to be hex-encoded (see WIN-2006 comment in apps/get_raw/[version]/[...path]/+page.svelte L7) — a literal `u/admin/<slug>` returns HTTP 500 `HexErr: Invalid character '/'`. Tests prove bundle round-trip via the structured GET /apps/get/p/<path> response (value.files['index.html'].code) instead of the raw bytes endpoint.

update_raw requires `value` in the `app` JSON for raw apps — update_app_internal only inserts a new app_version row when ns.value is Some, and the multipart handler then writes the JS bundle keyed by the new version id. Without `value` the new JS upload trips app_bundles_pkey and the request fails with HTTP 400.

features.name: Workers and worker groups
features.summary: Workers are background processes that pull jobs from the queue and execute scripts/flows. The /workers route renders a read-mostly admin page (CenteredPage + PageHeader 'Workers') that polls WorkerService.listWorkers({ perPage: 1000, pingSince: 300 }) every 5s and ConfigService.listWorkerGroups() in parallel, grouping pings by (worker_instance, worker_group) and rendering one Tabs entry per worker group with a per-tab `<n> worker(s)` count appended via the Tab `extra` snippet. The selected tab renders a WorkerGroup card (group config tags, memory cap, action buttons Edit config / Delete config / Clean cache / Restart workers) followed by an 'Active workers' DataTable with columns Worker | Worker tags? | Worker start | Jobs ran | Last job | Occupancy rate (15s/5m/30m/ever) | Memory usage (Windmill) | Limits | Version | Status | Repl, plus a TextInput placeholder `Search workers in group '<group>'` that calls filterWorkerGroupByNames (matches on worker name | worker_instance | ip). On the sandbox stack the well-known groups are `default` (3 alive workers named `wk-default-*`, broad language tags) and `native` (8 alive workers named `wk-native-*`, db-runtime tags). Most of the page's create/edit affordances (Manage tags, Queue metrics, New agent worker, New group config, Repl per worker, AutoscalingEvents) are gated on `$superadmin || $devopsRole` AND parts (autoscaling, the YAML import of group configs) are explicitly EE-gated. Backend: WorkerService.listWorkers → GET /api/workers/list, ConfigService.listWorkerGroups → GET /api/configs/list_worker_groups, WorkerService.getCustomTags → GET /api/w/<wid>/workers/custom_tags. Worker rows reflect server-side state (last_ping freshness drives the Alive/Dead Badge: isWorkerAlive = last_ping < 60); the page does not allow creating workers from the UI (workers register themselves on startup), so the CE-supported authored flows here are READ-ONLY assertions against the existing live workers.

provenance.note: Authored P1 from code + a live walk of /workers on the sandbox CE stack. The page renders an h1 'Workers' (PageHeader), worker-group Tabs whose accessible name is `<group-name> <n> worker(s)` (the pluralize() helper appends 'worker' / 'workers'), a WorkerGroup config card with action buttons (Edit / Delete / Clean cache / Restart), and a DataTable of active workers per selected group. The sandbox stack has groups: default (3 alive wk-default-*), native (8 alive wk-native-*), reports (0 alive). All scenarios here are READ-ONLY because (a) workers register themselves on startup — the UI offers no 'create worker' flow on CE, only superadmin-gated config edits, and (b) editing group configs, queue metrics, autoscaling, and the per-worker Repl are gated on $superadmin || $devopsRole and (autoscaling, YAML config export) are EE-gated. Tested directly against /workers; tests must wait for the 5s loadWorkers poll to populate the table after navigation.

vocabulary.workers-page: /workers; title 'Workers | Windmill' (set by +page.js stuff.title = 'Workers'); h1 'Workers' (PageHeader.svelte); CenteredPage layout.
vocabulary.workers-tabs: One Tab per worker group ({#each groupedWorkers.map((x) => x[0]) as name} in workers/+page.svelte L892). Each Tab's accessible name follows the pattern `<group-name> <n> worker(s)` because the Tab `extra` snippet (Tab.svelte L100–110) renders `pluralize(activeWorkers?.length, 'worker')` next to the label. Sandbox stack: 'default 3 workers', 'native 8 workers', 'reports 0 workers'.

Empty-result row renders the literal text 'No active workers found matching the search query' (L1250).
vocabulary.workers-status: Status cell renders a Badge with text 'Alive' (green, last_ping < 60s), 'Dead' (red, last_ping >= 60s), or 'Unknown' (gray, last_ping == null) followed by '<n>s ago' (L1200–1223). isWorkerAlive helper at L548–550.
vocabulary.workers-ee-and-superadmin-gates: Manage tags / Queue metrics / New agent worker / New group config buttons are conditional on `$superadmin || $devopsRole` (L605, 748–862). Per-worker 'Open repl' button is also superadmin/devops-gated (L984, 1224). Autoscaling events panel is wrapped in EE-only and only shown for non-agent groups (L1295–1297). YAML import of group configs is EE-gated.

corrections: /workers is the canonical URL; there is no /workers/list or /worker route. The page lives at frontend/src/routes/(root)/(logged)/workers/+page.svelte and its sibling +page.js sets the document title to 'Workers' via SvelteKit's stuff convention.

Worker GROUPS are configs (memory, tags, init scripts); per-group EDIT / DELETE / CLEAN-CACHE / RESTART buttons are visible to superadmins only. CE users without superadmin see the page and the read-only DataTable but cannot mutate group configs from the UI.

Workers cannot be CREATED from the UI — they register themselves on startup by POSTing a ping (workers/list reflects server state). The 'New agent worker' button on /workers opens an HttpAgentWorkerDrawer that issues a token; agent registration still happens out-of-band when the agent process starts. Authored UI flows here are therefore READ-ONLY assertions.

features.name: AI agents & assistants
features.summary: Windmill ships two distinct AI surfaces that share one configuration model: (a) the global 'Ask AI' chat panel — a right-side region with aria-label='AI chat' toggled by the sidebar 'Ask AI' MenuButton or the Cmd/Ctrl+L shortcut (svelte:window onkeydown listener in frontend/src/lib/components/copilot/chat/AIChat.svelte L110-118 calls aiChatManager.toggleOpen()); (b) 'AI Agent' flow modules — a FlowModuleValue::AIAgent { input_transforms, tools, tag, omit_output_from_conversation } variant declared in backend/windmill-types/src/flows.rs L966-973 that orchestrates a tools-calling LLM step inside a flow. Both surfaces are configured from the same workspace page: /workspace_settings?tab=ai mounts AISettings.svelte (frontend/src/lib/components/workspaceSettings/AISettings.svelte L36-613), which renders a SettingsPageHeader with title 'Windmill AI' + the canonical description, an 'AI Providers' SettingCard listing one Toggle per AI_PROVIDERS entry (frontend/src/lib/components/copilot/lib.ts L60-110: OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Anthropic (Recommended), Mistral, DeepSeek, Google AI, Groq, OpenRouter, Together AI, AWS Bedrock, Custom AI), plus cards for Default chat model, Metadata generation model, Code completion, Custom system prompts, and ModelTokenLimits. Saving wires each provider's resource_path through the admin-only POST /api/w/<wid>/edit_copilot_config (backend/windmill-api/src/workspaces.rs L89-167) into workspace_settings.ai_config. The chat panel mounts the AIChatDisplay region regardless of whether copilot is enabled — without a wired provider the panel renders a disabled hint linking back to /workspace_settings?tab=ai (AIChat.svelte L42-63), so the region's aria-label and the 'Chat' header / keyboard-hint footnote always render. All flows that actually invoke an LLM (sending a chat message, running an aiagent flow step) require a real provider key resource and are out of scope on this credential-less CE sandbox; CE-feasible flows here cover the chat panel mount, the workspace AI settings page render, and the persistence + flow-detail-page rendering of an aiagent flow module.

provenance.note: Authored P1 from code only — the chat panel mount and AI settings page render do not require any LLM credentials; the AI Agent flow module persistence + detail-page render is covered via the public POST /flows/create + GET /flows/get API + /flows/get/<path> SvelteKit page (no agent invocation). Any flow that actually invokes an LLM is explicitly distinct and marked skipReason='LLM-credentials required'.

The panel mounts regardless of provider configuration; without a wired provider it shows a disabled hint instead of accepting a prompt, but the region's chrome ('Chat' header + 'to open or close this chat' keyboard-hint footnote) still renders — so the mount path is fully testable on CE without any LLM credentials.

flows.title: Persist a flow that contains an AI Agent step

The backend deserializes via the 'aiagent' arm of the untagged FlowModuleValue deserializer (flows.rs L1104-1113); tools=[] is accepted (covered by the existing ai_agent_omit_output_from_conversation_defaults_to_false Rust unit test L1231-1245)

vocabulary.ai-keyboard-shortcut: svelte:window onkeydown in AIChat.svelte L110-118 — (e.ctrlKey || e.metaKey) && e.key === 'l' → aiChatManager.toggleOpen(). Playwright 'Control+l' fires ctrlKey+l on every platform; the handler accepts both ctrl and meta.
vocabulary.ai-settings-tab: /workspace_settings?tab=ai (sidebar entry id='ai' label='Windmill AI' at L1066-1070); page body branches on tab=='ai' (L1828-1844) and mounts <AISettings>.

vocabulary.ai-agent-flow-module: FlowModuleValue::AIAgent {input_transforms, tools, tag, omit_output_from_conversation} declared in backend/windmill-types/src/flows.rs L966-973; deserialize 'aiagent' arm at L1104-1113. Frontend factory createAiAgent() at flowStateUtils.svelte.ts L177-197 seeds input_transforms with provider / output_type / user_message. FlowGraphViewer renders the literal text 'AI Agent' for aiagent steps (FlowGraphViewerStep.svelte L144-145).

corrections: Windmill 'AI agents' is two distinct surfaces under one name: the global Ask AI chat panel (sidebar / Cmd-L) AND the AI Agent flow step type. They share configuration via workspace_settings.ai_config but render in totally different UIs.

The chat panel mounts the aria-label='AI chat' region regardless of whether copilot is enabled — without a wired provider the body renders a disabled hint instead of accepting prompts. The mount path is therefore testable on CE without LLM credentials; the prompt-and-reply path is not.

The aiagent flow module persists with tools=[] (covered by the Rust unit test ai_agent_omit_output_from_conversation_defaults_to_false at flows.rs L1231-1245). It is the LLM call at execution time — not the persistence — that requires a real provider key.

features.name: Audit logs
features.summary: Every workspace mutation (create/update/delete of scripts, flows, variables, schedules, users, folders, etc.) and every job execution writes one row into the audit_log table inside the same transaction as the mutation. The /audit_logs SvelteKit route (frontend/src/routes/(root)/(logged)/audit_logs/+page.svelte) renders an h1 'Audit logs' (L92), an AuditLogsFilters chrome (username / operation / action_kind / resource / before / after / scope / perPage), an AuditLogsTimeline pane, and an AuditLogsTable with five fixed columns: ID | Timestamp | Username | Operation | Resource (AuditLogsTable.svelte L131-143). On Community Edition the backend handlers in windmill-audit/src/audit_oss.rs are no-ops — audit_log() returns Ok(()), list_audit() returns vec![], get_audit() returns 404 'Audit log not available in Windmill Community edition'. On the running sandbox stack the EE binary is shipped but NO EE license key is wired in, so the backend lets rows through but masks each one: operation='redacted', resource='EE only', parameters={redacted:'-'}; the page therefore renders the warning Alert 'You need an enterprise license to see unredacted audit logs.' (audit_logs/+page.svelte L154-158) above the table. The id / timestamp / username / workspace_id / action_kind columns survive redaction and are the only fields that this feature's CE-feasible tests assert. Backend route: GET /api/w/{workspace}/audit/list (windmill-api/src/audit.rs L21+34); ListAuditLogQuery accepts username, operation, action_kind, resource, before, after, all_workspaces (windmill-audit/src/lib.rs L30-41). The action_kind filter is bound to the Postgres ACTION_KIND enum which is declared #[sqlx(rename_all = "lowercase")] (lib.rs L9) — callers must pass 'create' / 'update' / 'delete' / 'execute'; capitalised values fall through to PG and surface as a SqlErr. The Serialize side has no rename, so the JSON response writes 'Create' / 'Update' / 'Delete' / 'Execute'. Out-of-CE-scope features explicitly marked distinct: S3 archive export (GET /settings/audit_logs_s3_status — openapi.yaml L1686), unredacted operation/resource/parameters columns, AuditLogsTimeline graph drill-down. Operators are blocked from /audit_logs unless workspace.operator_settings.audit_logs is true (audit_logs/+page.svelte L83-87).

provenance.note: Authored P1 from code + a live API probe of /api/w/admins/audit/list on the sandbox CE stack. Rows come back redacted (operation='redacted', resource='EE only', parameters={redacted:'-'}) but the id / timestamp / username / workspace_id / action_kind columns are unredacted, so the authored tests pin the latter set as the CE invariant. The action_kind filter is lowercase-only (PG enum); capitalised values return SqlErr 'invalid input value for enum action_kind'. The /audit_logs page renders the EE alert + the full table chrome (h1 + five column headers) regardless of license, so the page-render flow is CE-feasible without any EE seeding.

flows.title: Filter audit logs by action_kind
flows.story: As an admin triaging recent activity, I filter the audit log by action_kind to narrow the result set to only Create / Update / Delete / Execute rows. The backend's ACTION_KIND Postgres enum is lowercase-only — the filter rejects 'Create' (capitalised) with a SqlErr — so the AuditLogsTable badge click handler must (and does) lowercase the value before re-fetching.
steps: Sign in as admin@windmill.dev

GET /api/w/admins/audit/list?per_page=20&action_kind=delete — every returned row's action_kind must be exactly 'Delete'
GET /api/w/admins/audit/list?action_kind=Create (uppercase) must fail — the PG enum returns 'invalid input value for enum action_kind' and the API bubbles 5xx

Then the response is 200 and every row's action_kind is 'Delete'
When I GET /api/w/admins/audit/list?action_kind=Create (capitalised)

vocabulary.audit-logs-page: /audit_logs (frontend/src/routes/(root)/(logged)/audit_logs/+page.svelte). h1 'Audit logs' (L92); +page.js does NOT override stuff.title, so document title falls through to the (logged) layout default 'Windmill'. AuditLogsTimeline pane (top h2/6) + SplitPanes (table on the left, AuditLogDetails drawer on the right).
vocabulary.audit-logs-table-columns: Five fixed columns when showWorkspace=false (default): ID | Timestamp | Username | Operation | Resource (AuditLogsTable.svelte L134-143). showWorkspace adds a 'Workspace' column between Username and Operation (rendered only when scope='instance' or 'all_workspaces').
vocabulary.audit-logs-redaction-alert: Warning Alert 'You need an enterprise license to see unredacted audit logs.' (audit_logs/+page.svelte L154-158). Rendered when !$enterpriseLicense || $enterpriseLicense.endsWith('_pro'). On the sandbox stack the EE binary is shipped but no license is wired, so the alert is always visible.
vocabulary.audit-logs-redacted-row: On CE-without-license each row comes back with operation='redacted', resource='EE only', parameters={redacted:'-'}. The id / timestamp / username / workspace_id / action_kind columns are preserved — these are the only fields safe to assert in CE tests.
vocabulary.audit-logs-action-kind-enum: ACTION_KIND Postgres enum, declared #[sqlx(type_name='ACTION_KIND', rename_all='lowercase')] (windmill-audit/src/lib.rs L9). Lowercase values 'create' / 'update' / 'delete' / 'execute' only — capitalised values fall through to PG as SqlErr 'invalid input value for enum action_kind'. The JSON response writes the un-renamed variant names 'Create' / 'Update' / 'Delete' / 'Execute'. The AuditLogsTable badge click handler lowercases the value before re-fetching (AuditLogsTable.svelte L237).
vocabulary.audit-list-endpoint: GET /api/w/{workspace}/audit/list — workspaced_service() in windmill-api/src/audit.rs L19-23. Accepts Pagination (page, per_page) + ListAuditLogQuery (username, operation, operations, exclude_operations, action_kind, resource, before, after, all_workspaces). Returns Vec<AuditLog> ordered by id DESC.

corrections: Audit logs are NOT a pure EE feature on the OSS backend either: the table exists in OSS migrations and the list endpoint is wired, but windmill-audit/src/audit_oss.rs no-ops audit_log() and list_audit() returns vec![] when the `private` cargo feature is OFF (the `cfg(not(feature = 'private'))` branches at L77-109). The full audit pipeline only fires when the EE binary is built; the sandbox stack uses the EE binary but masks rows without a license.

On CE-without-license rows have operation='redacted' and resource='EE only' — these are NOT generic placeholder strings, they are the exact masked values the backend writes. Tests that filter on operation / resource on CE will therefore only ever match the redacted sentinels.

The /audit_logs page title falls through to the layout default 'Windmill' — there is no `stuff.title = 'Audit logs'` override in +page.js. Tests that assert toHaveTitle(/Audit logs/) will hang for 30s and time out; assert /Windmill/ instead.

journeys.title: A new developer ships a scheduled Python script end-to-end
journeys.story: As a new developer, I sign in to Windmill, create a workspace, write a small Python script, run it from the auto-generated UI, schedule it on a cron, and verify the scheduled run appears in the runs history — so I can prove the platform supports the full author-to-schedule loop.

entry.summary: A fresh, signed-in developer with no workspace yet — lands on /user/workspaces.
entry.established: Auth is provisioned programmatically against the local Windmill backend (admin@windmill.dev / changeme; a Playwright storageState is reused so sign-in is not re-walked every run). The (logged) layout requires localStorage.workspace; without it every feature route redirects to /user/workspaces?rd=… — so workspace-create is the true first walked step.

steps.action: from /user/workspaces, click '+ Create a new workspace', then submit name 'Acme' and a unique workspace ID (e.g. 'acme-<rand>'); click 'Create workspace'
land on '/' (Workspace home) — the (logged) layout now has localStorage.workspace set, so feature routes are reachable

click the 'Script' CTA on home (CreateActionsScript); /scripts/add redirects to /scripts/edit/u/<owner>/draft_<uuid>; in the Language picker click 'Python', edit the body to `def main():\n    return 'hello windmill'`, then click 'Deploy'
deploy lands on /scripts/get/<scriptPath>; fill the auto-generated RunForm (no args), click the 'Run' button on the script-detail page
land on /run/<jobId>; verify the job's status = 'Success' and the result contains 'hello windmill'
back on /scripts/get/<scriptPath>, open the Triggers side-panel → Schedules → click '+ Schedule'; in the in-place modal set cron '* * * * *' and click 'Save'
navigate to /schedules (sidebar 'Schedules'); verify a row with the script's path is visible (filter chip 'All' or 'Enabled')
navigate to /runs (sidebar 'Runs'); verify a successful run for the scheduled script appears in the table

vocabulary.workspace-picker: h1 'Select a workspace'; existing-workspace button text 'Admins - admins as superadmin Used to manage your Windmill instance'; link '+ Create a new workspace' (href /user/create_workspace); title 'Workspace Selection | Windmill'
vocabulary.workspace-create: <CenteredModal> rendered on its own route (/user/create_workspace); fields 'Workspace name' (autofocus) and 'Workspace ID' (slug); optional 'Workspace color', 'AI key for Windmill AI', 'Auto-add anyone joining the instance'; submit button 'Create workspace'; back link '← Back to workspaces'; success → goto('/') and persists localStorage.workspace
vocabulary.home: title 'Home'; toggle 'Scripts | Flows | Apps' with hash '#workspace' and query '?kind=script'; sidebar links Home, Runs, Variables, Resources, Assets, Tutorials, Schedules (Triggers group), Workers; header CTAs labeled 'Script', 'Flow', 'App' (CreateActionsScript/Flow/App). NOTE: 'Script' is rendered with a11y role=link (href=/scripts/add), while 'Flow' and 'App' are role=button — locator must use getByRole('link', { name: /^Script$/ }) for the Script CTA
vocabulary.script-editor: /scripts/add immediately redirects to /scripts/edit/u/<owner>/draft_<uuid>?new_draft=true; side panel sections Metadata / Language / Script kind; default language 'TypeScript (Bun)'; path UI 'scripts / u/<owner> / <random-name>'; action buttons Diff, Settings, Deploy, Test (Ctrl+); seed admin owner segment is 'u/admin/...' (the username portion of the email is used, NOT the full email)
vocabulary.run-page: /scripts/get/<path> embeds RunForm + side 'Runs' button (→ /runs/<scriptPath>) + Triggers side-panel (containing Schedules sub-panel with '+ Schedule')
vocabulary.job-detail: /run/<jobId> page; h1 'run/<jobId>'; title 'Run <jobId> | Windmill'; has 'Edit schedule' button if job originated from a schedule
vocabulary.schedule-editor: true modal/drawer (ScheduleEditorInner) opened in place — URL does not change; section h2's: Metadata, Schedule, Runnable, Advanced; Croner reference link present
vocabulary.schedules-list: h1 'Schedules'; title 'Schedules | Windmill'; empty-state copy 'No schedules'; filter chips All / Enabled / Disabled; primary CTA 'New schedule'

corrections: spec previously had `home` at /; actual entry landing after sign-in is /user/workspaces — added a separate workspace-picker node
spec previously had workspace-create as a modal; it is its own route /user/create_workspace
spec previously had scripts-list as a separate route /scripts; /scripts returns 404 — scripts are a tab on / (?kind=script#workspace) reached via the home page itself, so scripts-list was removed
spec previously had `home → scripts-list` via a 'Scripts' nav link; no such sidebar link exists. The actual edge is `home → script-editor` via the 'Script' CTA

spec previously had run-page at /run/:path; the auto-generated run UI lives at /scripts/get/:path. /run/:jobId is the job-detail page, keyed by jobId — split into run-page (/scripts/get/:path) and job-detail (/run/:jobId)
spec previously had `run-page → schedule-editor` via 'schedule this script' link; actual UX is the in-place Triggers side-panel → Schedules → +Schedule on /scripts/get/:path
spec previously had `schedules-list → runs-history` via 'view runs' link; no per-schedule link exists — navigation is via sidebar Runs

gherkin.text:
@journey @e2e
Feature: A new developer ships a scheduled Python script end-to-end
  Background:
    Given a signed-in developer with no workspace yet, landed on /user/workspaces

  Scenario: From workspace creation to a scheduled run
    When I create a workspace named "Acme" with a unique id              # produces: workspaceId
    And I land on the workspace home at /                                # consumes: workspaceId
    And I create a Python script that returns "hello windmill" and deploy # produces: scriptPath
    And I trigger the script from its auto-generated run UI              # produces: jobId
    And the job-detail page shows the run completed successfully         # consumes: jobId
    And I open the Triggers panel and schedule the script every minute    # produces: scheduleId
    Then the schedule appears under Schedules                             # consumes: scheduleId
    And the runs history shows the scheduled run completed successfully   # consumes: jobId

journeys.title: An ops engineer composes a resource+variable-driven flow and fires it via an HTTP route
journeys.story: As an ops-minded developer, I add a Resource and a Variable, author a Python script that consumes both, compose a 2-step flow that calls that script, expose the flow via an HTTP route, fire the route, and inspect the resulting job — so I can prove the full configure → compose → trigger → inspect loop works end-to-end.

entry.summary: A signed-in admin in the seeded 'admins' workspace, with at least one deployed Python script already available in the workspace (preconditioned by the variables-and-resources / scripts feature walks).
entry.established: Auth is provisioned programmatically against the local Windmill backend (admin@windmill.dev / changeme) by helpers/userApi.loginAdmin; a Playwright storageState is reused across specs so sign-in is not re-walked. The (logged) layout requires localStorage.workspace = 'admins'; this is also seeded in the storageState so feature routes are reachable directly without going through /user/workspaces.

steps.action: From Home, navigate to /resources (sidebar Resources); click 'New resource', pick (or create) a simple resource_type (e.g. 'c_config'), fill path 'u/admin/ops-cfg-<rand>' and a JSON value like {"region":"eu-west-1"}; click Save — a row referencing the new resource path appears in the list
Navigate to /variables (sidebar Variables); click 'New variable', fill path 'u/admin/ops-var-<rand>' with value 'prod' (Secret OFF); click Save — a row with the new variable path is visible

From Home, click the 'Script' CTA — /scripts/add redirects to /scripts/edit/u/admin/draft_<uuid>; pick Python; replace the body with `import wmill

def main():
    cfg = wmill.get_resource('<resourcePath>')
    env = wmill.get_variable('<variablePath>')
    return {'region': cfg['region'], 'env': env}`; click Deploy — land on /scripts/get/<scriptPath>
steps.walkNote: Body references both wmill.get_resource(resourcePath) and wmill.get_variable(variablePath); the walk may API-deploy this script (POST /api/w/admins/scripts/create) and only UI-assert /scripts/get/<scriptPath> to keep the arc resilient — Monaco body-replace is the brittle part.

steps.action: From Home, click the 'Flow' CTA, pick 'Flow Editor' — navigate to /flows/edit/u/admin/draft_<uuid>?new_draft=true; from the FlowBuilder graph, add an Action step that calls the deployed <scriptPath> (Script picker, inputs auto-wired from the script's schema); add a second inline Python Action step that echoes the previous step's result; click Deploy — land on /flows/get/<flowPath>
steps.walkNote: API-driven mutation likely — the FlowBuilder drag-drop / module-picker UI is fragile. The walk may POST /api/w/admins/flows/create with a hand-authored FlowValue (two-step modules array) and only UI-assert /flows/get/<flowPath>'s FlowGraphViewer renders two nodes.

steps.action: Land on /flows/get/<flowPath>; verify the FlowGraphViewer shows the 2-step graph and the auto-generated RunForm is rendered — confirms the flow is deployed and ready to be wired into a trigger
Navigate to /routes (sidebar TRIGGERS → 'HTTP'); confirm PageHeader title 'Custom HTTP routes'; click 'New route' — the RouteEditor drawer opens

In the RouteEditor drawer, under 'Target' switch the runnable picker to Flow and pick <flowPath>; under 'HTTP' set route_path 'ops-<rand>' and leave http_method on 'POST'; click Save — the drawer closes and a row with route_path 'ops-<rand>' is visible at /routes
From the test harness, POST to /api/r/admins/<routePath> (workspaced) with an empty JSON body; expect 2xx and a returned jobId — confirms the route resolved to the flow and enqueued a job
steps.walkNote: Step is API-driven (fetch / curl from inside the spec) — there is no /routes UI affordance to fire a route directly; the UI assertion lives in the next two steps.

steps.action: Navigate to /runs (sidebar Runs); verify a row for the flow at <flowPath> with a recent timestamp and a successful status icon is present in the runs table

Click the row (or navigate to /run/<jobId>); verify the FlowProgressBar / FlowStatusViewer shows both steps with Success status icons and the 'Result' panel surfaces {'region': 'eu-west-1', 'env': 'prod'} — proves the resource + variable flowed through the script through the flow through the route

concessions: Steps 4 (script body) and 5 (flow composition) are flagged with walkNote; the walk-journey phase may API-author these mutations and only UI-assert the resulting /scripts/get/<path> and /flows/get/<path> pages, because Monaco body-replace + FlowBuilder drag-drop are the most fragile parts of the Windmill UI and the journey's arc is the configure → compose → trigger → inspect loop, not the act of typing code into Monaco.
Step 8 (firing the route) has no /routes UI affordance — it is intentionally API-driven (fetch from the spec) with the UI assertion deferred to /runs and /run/<jobId>.
openQuestions: Does the seeded 'admins' workspace already have a 'c_config' resource_type, or does the walk need to create one first? If the latter, step 1 may need a 'pick / create resource type' sub-step.
Does POST /api/r/admins/<routePath> require an api_token header in this Community Edition sandbox, or does the storageState cookie authenticate the call? The walk will confirm.
